# cloudflare_load_balancer_monitor_group (Resource)



## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "cloudflare_load_balancer_monitor_group" "example_load_balancer_monitor_group" {
  account_id = "023e105f4ecef8ad9ca31a8372d0c353"
  description = "Primary datacenter monitors"
  members = [{
    enabled = true
    monitor_id = "monitor_id"
    monitoring_only = false
    must_be_healthy = true
  }]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `account_id` (String) Identifier.
- `description` (String) A short description of the monitor group
- `members` (Attributes List) List of monitors in this group (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--members))

### Read-Only

- `created_on` (String) The timestamp of when the monitor group was created
- `id` (String) The ID of the Monitor Group to use for checking the health of origins within this pool.
- `modified_on` (String) The timestamp of when the monitor group was last updated

<a id="nestedatt--members"></a>
### Nested Schema for `members`

Required:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether this monitor is enabled in the group
- `monitor_id` (String) The ID of the Monitor to use for checking the health of origins within this pool.
- `monitoring_only` (Boolean) Whether this monitor is used for monitoring only (does not affect pool health)
- `must_be_healthy` (Boolean) Whether this monitor must be healthy for the pool to be considered healthy

Read-Only:

- `created_at` (String) The timestamp of when the monitor was added to the group
- `updated_at` (String) The timestamp of when the monitor group member was last updated

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
$ terraform import cloudflare_load_balancer_monitor_group.example '<account_id>/<monitor_group_id>'
```
